title: clinopodium atlanticum
intro: Clinopodium atlanticum, also known as Atlantic Clinopodium or Wild Basil, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, colds, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and expectorant properties. Clinopodium atlanticum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or essential oil, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
